title: glochidion oxycarpum
intro: Glochidion oxycarpum, also known as the Indian gall or Indian cheese tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve various gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Glochidion oxycarpum include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and infections. Traditional herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and poultices made from the plant's leaves, which can be consumed or applied topically to experience its medicinal effects.
